other_observers: None; eBird shows six records for a Least Bittern at Holcim Wetlands this summer, including mine.
specific_location: The Holcim Wetlands. Located on CR 120 about 1 mile west of the Holcim Cement plant. Open to the public.
description: Very small heron-type bird. Seen briefly in flight. Light chestnut-colored coverts on dark wings. Dark back. Dark crown. Yellow legs.Behaviors: The bittern was flushed from the shoreline of the cattail marsh. It quickly flew low over the cattails and dropped back into the marsh. Not seen again that day.Call: Made a loud squawk when flushed.Plumage: Adult or juvenile
